criteria.description: The stardust:prototype skill was invoked and Discipline 11 was applied at craft time.
The proposed file's <style> block opens with /* === GLOBAL: tokens === */ immediately preceding the :root declaration.
An /* === GLOBAL: resets === */ marker precedes the html/body/main element selectors.
An /* === GLOBAL: default-content === */ marker precedes the section > <el> default-content rules; all default-content rules use the direct-child combinator.
When canon.css applies, an /* === GLOBAL: compound utility === */ marker precedes canon.css classes (.btn-primary, .card, .link, form inputs).
When cinematic motion applies, an /* === GLOBAL: motion === */ marker precedes @keyframes definitions; motion tokens land under GLOBAL: tokens (no fifth scope introduced).

Given a page-specific class selector with no scoping prefix (e.g., .headline { ... } not under GLOBAL: compound utility), the validator refuses the write with a structured message naming the selector, line number, and cross-link to Discipline 11.
The refusal message offers the four remediation options (compound utility / section-scoped / block-scoped / default-content) verbatim per the Discipline 11 spec.
No mention of EDS, AEM, localhost:3000, dev servers, or framework targets in outputs or narration.
